guide_response.verdict: APPROVED_WITH_AMENDMENTS_BOUND -- Stage 5.0.v2 dual-track pre-flight audit checkpoint binds 14 spec corrections (Items 5.0.5 through 5.11 + 5.3 atomicity + Pre-Flight Step 0 rollback-readiness per Guide-#8 dispatch 9e92b26) under one new acknowledgment g9-ea9_n1 with sub-clauses (a) through (n). Sub-clause (e) is the Class A LOAD-BEARING amendment converting Item 5.5.v2 wholesale `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance` to a SELECTIVE 4-of-17 delete preserving the 13 BlarAI-internal architectural governance docs per Anomaly A14 disposition. Class C amendments (8) prevent EA-9 STOP at Item 5.9 (disabled-task probe) and complete the Item 5.4 audit scope per aug1 + Item 5.10 HALT-gate review matrix per aug2 + Item 5.6 testpaths enumeration per r4 + Item 5.7 .gitignore concrete additions per w20 + Item 5.8 stale-baseline supersede + Item 5.3 atomicity rollback per r2 + Pre-Flight Step 0 rollback-readiness verification per Guide-#8 dispatch (n1(n); 5 critical checks ~30s, HARD STOP on any failure). Class D amendments (5) are audit-trail completeness items required per LA mature-not-minimal motto. EA-9 may begin Stage 5 execution at Pre-Flight Step 0 (rollback-readiness verification) → Step 1 (state.pause_fleet on devplatform) → Item 5.0.5 after restating g9-ea9_n1 verbatim with all 14 sub-clauses + the LA pre-prep checklist (in §required_acknowledgments). 4 forward-carry tickets bound for Stage 6.7.5 (NN skipped per skip-the-cancelled-letter convention): MM (BlarAI_daily_digest doc-defect; dispatch posterity correction; iter-1), OO (fleet_observability auto-run policy; LA architectural), PP (vikunja-autostart-shortcut.md doc update; subagent §23 MM; renamed from NN), QQ (13 BlarAI-internal governance docs review post-Stage-5; subagent §23 NN; renamed from PP; A14 follow-up). Stage 6.7.5 backlog: 25 tickets total. Cumulative-ack chain at EA-9 spawn: 116 inherited + 14 new = **130 binding atoms**.

summary.trigger_context:
Stage 4 OPERATIONALLY CLOSED 2026-04-29 (commit `ecc8e7c` on `chore/platform-extraction`; HEAD has advanced to `f63eb3f` post-handoff via 2 LA-correction commits + 1 residual cleanup commit). Stage 5 (Cleanup, MEDIUM risk, single_session) is next; 12+4 v2 work items in 06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml. LA chose F.4 dual-track audit posture (Option A subagent + Option B+ inline reconciliation) per the upgraded "mature not minimal" recalibration captured in `feedback_recalibrate_against_mature_not_minimal.md` (~60-75 min total pre-flight). Phase C lesson — 5 bugs surfacing at "MEDIUM"-labeled Stage 4 — drove the dual-track choice; this audit's Class A finding (g9-ea9_n1(e)) vindicates it.
Guide-#9 spawned ONE general-purpose audit subagent (agent ID `a01e48be5f5692070`; read-only mandate; ~14 min execution; 84 tool uses; ~228k tokens; coverage estimate ~85% — above LA's 80% target) AND performed Option B+ inline reconciliation cross-verifying critical findings on disk per dn14/dn20/dn24/A18. Audit subagent evidence committed alongside this verdict at: `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/5.0.v2-AUDIT_SUBAGENT_OUTPUT_STAGE5_PREFLIGHT_DRIFT_INVENTORY.md` (939 lines).
summary.approach:
Audit subagent inventoried all 16 Stage 5 sub-items against actual disk state. Per-item drift classification (NONE / STRUCTURE_MISMATCH / NEW_PATTERN / FILE_NOT_FOUND / CASCADE / KNOWN) + severity (Class A/B/C/D) + concrete recommended disposition. aug1 8-glob audit completed end-to-end with Grep + classification per glob (live-code vs comment vs test-fixture vs forensic). aug3 Stage 5.5/5.5.v2 dry-run simulation per pattern with file enumeration + dn13 verification (NOT-REAL confirmed — non-recursive `Remove-Item` glob behavior preserves `temp_for_responses/`).
Guide-#9 (Option B+ inline) performed independent disk cross-verifications on the highest-impact subagent findings (per rc3 subagent verification discipline + dn14/dn20/dn24/A18): - **g9-ea9_n1(e) Class A**: `Glob` of `BlarAI/docs/governance/*` returned 17 files (README.md, STYLE.md, circuit-breaker.md, configuration-management.md, context-spotlighting.md, deployment-verification.md, error-recovery.md, fleet-hygiene.md, gpu-runtime.md, ipc-protocol.md, merge-policy.md, observability.md, parallel-sprints.md, pgov-validation.md, rule-engine.md, session-state.md, streaming-output.md). `Glob` of `devplatform/docs/governance/*` returned only 4 (STYLE.md, fleet-hygiene.md, merge-policy.md, parallel-sprints.md per A14). The 13 not migrated would be wholesale-deleted by current dispatch line 30 `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force`. **CONFIRMED.** - **g9-ea9_n1(g) Class C**: Read of `BlarAI/pyproject.toml` `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` testpaths (lines 6-22) — exactly 15 testpaths matching subagent's 9 PRESERVE + 6 REMOVE enumeration. Markers (lines 24-27): `asyncio` + `slow`, no platform-tool refs. **CONFIRMED.** - **g9-ea9_n1(h) Class C**: Read of `BlarAI/.gitignore` (138 lines) — `tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/` already ignored (line 137); `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` NOT yet ignored; `.platform/audit_archive/*.zip` and `pre_stage5_*/` NOT yet ignored. **CONFIRMED.** - **aug3 dn13-violation r3**: Independent Glob check confirms `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/CLAUDE_*` / `CO_LEAD_*` / `DEC*` / `DOMAIN*` / `CONTINUATION_PROMPT.md` all return zero matches. PowerShell `Remove-Item` non-recursive behavior on top-level `docs/CLAUDE_*.md` etc. patterns is verified safe. **r3 CONFIRMED NOT-REAL.**
Other findings accepted on subagent's per-item disk-verification record per audit MD §1-§16. No subagent claim was bound into this verdict without disk-level cross-check on the Class A and load-bearing Class C items.
summary.option_b_plus_inline_reconciliation: Per LA F.4 dual-track posture, Guide-#9's Option B+ inline reconciliation was scoped to aug1-aug3 surfaces. The audit subagent absorbed aug1 (8-glob expansion verification with full grep classification) and aug3 (per-pattern dry-run simulation) into its own §18 reports; Option B+ contribution was: (i) Independent disk verification of the Class A finding (governance dir composition both sides) — confirmed. (ii) Independent disk verification of pyproject.toml testpaths enumeration — confirmed. (iii) Independent disk verification of .gitignore current state — confirmed. (iv) aug2 HALT-gate amendment text drafted by subagent §18 + §15; Guide-#9 reviewed and accepts as-is (subagent's matrix format with [PASS/FAIL] placeholders is operationally clean for LA review at Item 5.10 gate). Total Option B+ time: ~20 min (file reads + Glob enumeration + cross-check synthesis). Combined with subagent's ~14 min: **~34 min total pre-flight** (under LA's 60-75 min ceiling; the dual-track was net-efficient because subagent absorbed most aug1+aug3 surface).
summary.findings_overview:
14 amendments bound under g9-ea9_n1 sub-clauses (a) through (n). Class breakdown: - **Class A (data-loss / irreversible): 1 sub-clause** ((e) Item 5.5.v2 selective-vs-wholesale governance delete). LOAD-BEARING. - **Class B (duplication / functional regression): 0 sub-clauses.** - **Class C (functional regression / EA STOP risk): 8 sub-clauses** ((c) Item 5.4 8-glob expansion; (g) Item 5.6 testpaths enumeration; (h) Item 5.7 concrete .gitignore additions; (i) Item 5.8 supersede-by-5.8.v2; (j) Item 5.9 F.2 mini-soak replacement; (k) Item 5.10 HALT-gate review matrix; (m) Item 5.3 atomicity rollback; (n) Pre-Flight Step 0 rollback-readiness verification per Guide-#8 dispatch 9e92b26). - **Class D (cosmetic / audit-trail; non-optional per LA mature-not-minimal): 5 sub-clauses** ((a) Item 5.0.5 archive pattern extension; (b) Item 5.1.v2 resolver path correction; (d) Item 5.5 .md peers; (f) Item 5.5.5 ea_queue path; (l) Item 5.11 tag-ordering guard).
Re-counting precisely: A=1, B=0, C=8, D=5 (totals=14). The audit MD §22 listed 13 sub-clauses (a)-(m) at C=6 + D=8 based on subagent's classification choices; this verdict adopts the audit's per-letter assignment (a)-(m) verbatim and adds (n) for the Guide-#8 rollback-readiness Pre-Flight Step 0 dispatch (folded in per LA directive 2026-04-29 in lieu of a 4th audit pass).
4 items pass without amendment: 5.2 (idempotent .platform/ creation), 5.8.v2 (correct as-is), 5.11 (substantive amend at (l) is ordering-guard only; tag commands themselves correct), 5.12 (template per Stage 3/4 closure pattern; hash-verify discipline carries forward per d9).
All 14 amendments REQUIRED. No sub-clause is optional. Per LA mature-not-minimal motto + memory feedback: hardening followups are non-optional, both documentation AND architectural cleanup. The same standard applies to the 5 Class D items in this verdict — "cosmetic" does not mean "skippable".
summary.new_acknowledgment_summary: g9-ea9_n1 sub-clauses bind the following amendments (full text in §new_acknowledgment below; line refs are 06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml): - **n1(a)** Item 5.0.5 (line 96): extend `$patterns` array to include `'lifts.json', 'escalation_seen.json'` (Class D; F.1 Option A "preserves stragglers" framing) - **n1(b)** Item 5.1.v2 (line 14): correct resolver path `tools\fleet_ops\` → `tools\autonomy_budget\` per Workflow-Integrity-Review S-1 / R12 (Class D) - **n1(c)** Item 5.4 (lines 187-189): EXPAND `` from 3 globs to 8 per aug1 + add audit-acceptance criteria in description (Class C) - **n1(d)** Item 5.5 (lines 199-206): extend `` to add `docs\DOMAIN*.md` + `docs\domain*.md` (lowercase) + forward-compat `docs\DEC*.md` (Class D) - **n1(e)** Item 5.5.v2 (lines 23-34): **REPLACE wholesale `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance` with SELECTIVE 4-of-17 delete preserving 13 BlarAI-internal docs** per A14 disposition. KEEP wake_templates wholesale-delete (6/6 fully migrated). **Class A LOAD-BEARING.** - **n1(f)** Item 5.5.5 (line 42): correct path-spec `docs/ea_queue/archive/` → `scheduled/ea_queue/archive/` (cosmetic; Class D) - **n1(g)** Item 5.6 (line 211): replace description "remove anything referencing tools/vikunja_mcp, tools/fleet_*, tools/autonomy_budget" with explicit 6-REMOVE + 9-PRESERVE testpaths enumeration (Class C; r4) - **n1(h)** Item 5.7 (line 215): replace vague description with concrete .gitignore additions: `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` (per w20), `.platform/audit_archive/*.zip`, `.platform/audit_archive/pre_stage5_*/` (Class C) - **n1(i)** Item 5.8 (line 220): mark SUPERSEDED-BY-5.8.v2; replace `` with v2 baseline (~981 passed / 22 skipped on `pytest shared/ services/ launcher/`) (Class C) - **n1(j)** Item 5.9 (lines 226-230): REPLACE original `Start-ScheduledTask -TaskName "BlarAI_daily_digest"` (DISABLED + wrong-name) with F.2 30-min mini-soak protocol observing 8 ACTIVE tasks per their cadence (Class C; F.2 binding) - **n1(k)** Item 5.10 (lines 240-248): EXTEND HALT-message block with explicit 4-row pre-merge review matrix [1] 5.4 audit (8 globs PASS/FAIL), [2] 5.8.v2 baseline (~981/22 PASS/FAIL), [3] 5.9 mini-soak (firing-task LastTaskResult=0 PASS/FAIL; non-firing tasks NOT failure), [4] DIFF SUMMARY (Class C; aug2 binding) - **n1(l)** Item 5.11 (lines 254-257): add precondition guard checking `git branch --show-current` is `main` post-5.10 merge; warn (not throw) if still on `chore/platform-extraction` (Class D) - **n1(m)** Item 5.3 (lines 167-174): wrap `foreach ($m in $moves)` loop in try/catch with rollback list `$applied`; on mid-loop failure, reverse the applied moves before re-throwing (Class C; r2 atomicity) - **n1(n)** Pre-Flight Step 0 (NEW; not in original dispatch): rollback-readiness verification per Guide-#8 dispatch `5.0-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_8_ROLLBACK_READINESS_PREFLIGHT_FOR_EA_9.xml` (committed at 9e92b26). 5 critical checks (~30s runtime); HARD STOP on any failure; LA decision required for recovery (regenerate tag/bundle/OOG-zip/Phase-C-fix). EA-9 captures output in Stage 5.12 STATUS append (Class C; rollback parachute integrity; folded in per LA directive 2026-04-29 in lieu of a 4th audit pass)
summary.forward_carry:
**To Stage 5 EA-9 in-execution (4 items)**: - **EX-1 (LOAD-BEARING)**: Per g9-ea9_n1(e) Class A finding, EA-9 MUST execute n1(e)'s selective-delete amendment at Item 5.5.v2 (preserving 13 BlarAI-internal governance docs per A14), NOT the original dispatch wholesale-delete. If at execution time the dispatch text appears unamended, EA-9 STOPS and verifies with LA before proceeding. Data-preservation default if LA unreachable: selective-delete (preserve the 13 BlarAI-internal docs); rationale: "irreversible operations require explicit affirmation". (EX-1 wording corrected post-emission for parity with corrected ack9; same logical fix applied across both surfaces.) - **EX-2 (F.2 timing)**: Per g9-ea9_n1(j), EA-9 must run the F.2 30-min mini-soak ONLY after Item 5.7 commit lands; the soak window is 30 min real time. Plan session pacing accordingly. EA-9 is on a single-session block; the soak adds 30 min to the typical Stage 5 duration (~3-4h total). - **EX-3 (HALT-gate matrix)**: Per g9-ea9_n1(k), the Item 5.10 HALT-gate review matrix MUST be filled in by EA-9 with actual PASS/FAIL values (not `` placeholders) before printing the HALT message. EA-9 determines actual values from the Item 5.4/5.8.v2/5.9 outcomes. - **EX-4 (STATUS append discipline)**: Per d9 watchpoint + A17, EA-9's Item 5.12 STATUS append must be hash-verified pre-vs-post for duplication BEFORE committing. Use `Add-Content -NoNewline` or hash-verified append helper (per Stage 6.7.5 ticket pending investigation).
**To Stage 6.7.5 hardening tickets (4 NEW; NN skipped per skip-the-cancelled-letter convention)**: - **TICKET MM (NEW, LOW priority)** (carryforward from iter-1): 06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml dispatch text posterity-corrections — Item 5.9 references task name `BlarAI_daily_digest` (underscore form); real Title Case Spaces name per CLAUDE.md + Stage 4 evidence is `Daily Digest`. Moot at Stage 5 (per n1(j) full Item 5.9 replacement) but the dispatch text remains uncorrected. Optional posterity-edit post-procedure for traceability. - **TICKET NN — SKIPPED**: cancelled per LA preference 2026-04-29; was the permanent-decommission ticket for the 5 currently-Disabled fleet_observability reports (Welcome Back Poll, Daily Digest, Weekly Summary, Dashboard Maintainer, Toast Watchdog). Superseded by Ticket OO architectural policy (5 tasks remain Disabled but NOT decommissioned; re-enabling requires LA pre-approval). Skip-the-cancelled-letter convention applied to prevent posterity-lookup confusion across the audit chain. - **TICKET OO (NEW, MED priority)** (LA architectural): "No auto-running reports / digests / dashboards in fleet_observability without explicit LA pre-approval." Document in `devplatform/docs/governance/fleet-hygiene.md` or new `report-policy.md`. Prevents regression of Daily-Digest-style tasks. The 5 currently-Disabled tasks (Welcome Back Poll, Daily Digest, Weekly Summary, Dashboard Maintainer, Toast Watchdog) remain Disabled per LA preference (NOT decommissioned); re-enabling any of them requires LA pre-approval per this policy. - **TICKET PP (NEW, MED priority)** (subagent §23 MM; renamed from NN per skip-the-cancelled-letter convention): vikunja-autostart-shortcut.md doc update — `devplatform/tools/scheduled-tasks/vikunja-autostart-shortcut.md` lines 18, 22 reference `BlarAI\tools\vikunja` paths that get deleted at Stage 5.1. Update doc to reference `devplatform\tools\vikunja` post-Stage-5. - **TICKET QQ (NEW, LOW priority)** (subagent §23 NN; renamed from PP per skip-the-cancelled-letter convention; A14 follow-up): 13 BlarAI-internal governance docs review — post-Stage-5.5.v2 selective delete (per n1(e)), the 13 retained docs (README.md, circuit-breaker.md, configuration-management.md, context-spotlighting.md, deployment-verification.md, error-recovery.md, gpu-runtime.md, ipc-protocol.md, observability.md, pgov-validation.md, rule-engine.md, session-state.md, streaming-output.md) should be reviewed at Stage 6 for whether each ACTUALLY belongs in BlarAI or follows the 4 fleet-relevant ones to devplatform.
**Stage 6.7.5 backlog at Stage 5 EA-9 spawn time (25 tickets total)**: - 20 inherited from Stages 0-4: I, K, L, M, V, W, X, Y, Z, AA, BB, CC, EE, FF, GG, HH, II, JJ, KK, LL - 1 carryforward: TICKET A (Stage 3 EA-1 vs EA-7 naming defect per A12) - 4 NEW from this Guide-#9 cycle: MM, OO, PP, QQ (NN skipped per skip-the-cancelled-letter convention) Total: **25 tickets**. (Note: the audit subagent's MD §23 used local letters MM/NN/OO/PP for its 4 ticket recommendations; this verdict's final binding letters MM/OO/PP/QQ resolve them per LA's option (a) renaming. Subagent §23's local "OO" (Stage 5.5 pattern symmetry / 3 .md peers) is absorbed into n1(d) at Stage 5; subagent §23's local "PP" (Stage 5.0.5 pattern extension / 2 runtime files) is absorbed into n1(a) at Stage 5; no separate Stage 6.7.5 tickets needed for those.)
summary.residual_risk_acknowledgment:
Audit subagent estimated ~85% drift coverage (above LA's 80% target). Coverage gaps per audit MD §21: - Pytest dry-run not performed (read-only mandate forbids; 5.8/5.8.v2 verified spec-vs-baseline only). - Item 5.10 merge-conflict probability against live `main` not tested. - Item 5.11 tag creation not dry-run-tested. - Item 5.12 STATUS.md surgical-append not pre-validated against AppendAllText behavior (per d9 / A17). - Live wake_launcher firing during the 30-min mini-soak (Item 5.9 F.2) not observed.
5 likely-to-surface items during execution per audit MD §24: 1. PowerShell `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force` on `BlarAI/tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/` may hit filesystem-lock errors if a wake_launcher firing is concurrent. Mitigation: EA-9 pauses the fleet at Stage 5 entry per fleet-pause SOP (Pre-Flight per copilot-instructions §fleet_pause_sop). 2. Phase C revert state of `BlarAI/.mcp.json` (literal password) — currently uncommitted, gitignored per Ticket I. At Item 5.10 `git add -A`, this file is excluded by .gitignore; behavior verified safe. 3. STATUS.md duplication recurrence per d9; AppendAllText behavior root-cause unknown; EA-9 hash-verifies pre-vs-post. 4. Test baseline drift between audit and execution — 5.8.v2 expects ~981/22; intervening commits may shift count. 5. devplatform `0e81181` is the unpause commit — fleet is RUNNING at audit time. EA-9 Pre-Flight pause MUST happen before any branch checkout or destructive edit; LA does NOT need to coordinate the pause (EA-9 handles per fleet-pause SOP).
Defense-in-depth posture: triple-subagent verification at Stage 5 close (per d6/w14) catches drift this checkpoint missed. EA-9's per-item fail-fast gates + the dispatch's Level-2 rollback (`git reset --hard pre-platform-extract`) remain the architectural backstop.

independent_disk_verification.audit_provenance:
Audit performed by general-purpose subagent (agent ID `a01e48be5f5692070`; spawned 2026-04-29 by Guide-#9). Read-only mandate; zero file mutations except writing the single output MD at the prescribed path. Subagent observed: - BlarAI HEAD `f63eb3f` on `chore/platform-extraction` (working tree: `M tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` BUG-5 dead-artifact + `?? .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` + 3 untracked Stage-5.0 calibration XMLs at `temp_for_responses/`) - devplatform HEAD `0e81181` on `main` (working tree: 3 untracked artifacts per handoff worktree_state_devplatform) - state.json sha256 (BlarAI = BUG-5 emergency-pause snapshot, dead artifact; devplatform = `fleet_paused=false` per `last_updated_by=guide_8_handoff` at `2026-04-29T15:45:00Z`) - 13 scheduled tasks at TaskPath `\BlarAI\` — 8 ACTIVE + 5 DISABLED per Phase A 2026-04-29 - All 16 Stage 5 items verified per dispatch line ranges (audit MD §1-§16) - aug1 8 globs verified clean (audit MD §18 aug1) - aug3 6 patterns dry-run with file enumeration (audit MD §18 aug3)
Full subagent output at `temp_for_responses/5.0.v2-AUDIT_SUBAGENT_OUTPUT_STAGE5_PREFLIGHT_DRIFT_INVENTORY.md` (939 lines).
independent_disk_verification.guide_9_independent_cross_verifications:
Per dn14/dn20/dn24/A18 + rc3 subagent verification discipline: never accept subagent claim about file state without independent disk cross-check. Guide-#9 cross-verified the 3 highest-impact subagent findings on disk:
| Finding | Subagent claim | Guide-#9 cross-check | Verdict | |---|---|---|---| | g9-ea9_n1(e) Class A — 5.5.v2 wholesale-delete | BlarAI/docs/governance/ has 17 files; only 4 migrated to devplatform; 13 not migrated | Glob `docs/governance/*` BlarAI side returns 17 files (including README, circuit-breaker, configuration-management, context-spotlighting, deployment-verification, error-recovery, gpu-runtime, ipc-protocol, observability, pgov-validation, rule-engine, session-state, streaming-output, plus the 4 migrated ones); Glob devplatform side returns 4 (STYLE.md, fleet-hygiene.md, merge-policy.md, parallel-sprints.md). | **CONFIRMED** | | g9-ea9_n1(g) Class C — 5.6 testpaths | 9 PRESERVE + 6 REMOVE per pyproject.toml | Read of BlarAI/pyproject.toml lines 6-22: exact match — 9 PRESERVE (shared/tests + 5 services/* tests + launcher/tests + tests/integration + tools/tests) + 6 REMOVE (vikunja_mcp/bridge/tests + vikunja_mcp/tests + autonomy_budget/tests + gate_stale_cleaner/tests + fleet_observability/tests + fleet_ops/tests). Markers: asyncio + slow only; no platform-tool refs. | **CONFIRMED** | | g9-ea9_n1(h) Class C — 5.7 .gitignore | `tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/` already ignored; `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` not yet ignored; `.platform/audit_archive/` not yet ignored | Read of BlarAI/.gitignore (138 lines): line 137 `tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/` ✓; no `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` entry ✓; no `.platform/audit_archive/` entry ✓. | **CONFIRMED** | | r3 dn13-violation NOT-REAL | 6 patterns scoped to docs/ top-level; non-recursive Remove-Item; temp_for_responses/ safe | Independent Glob of docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/{CLAUDE_*, CO_LEAD_*, DEC*, DOMAIN*, CONTINUATION_PROMPT.md} all return 0 matches; PowerShell `Remove-Item docs\CLAUDE_*.md` is non-recursive by default; preserve clause line 207 explicit. | **CONFIRMED NOT-REAL** |
Coverage of cross-verification: 3 of 4 highest-impact findings verified on disk (the 4th was a NEGATIVE finding — r3 NOT-REAL — verified by structural argument + independent Glob). The remaining 9 sub-clauses (a, b, d, f, i, j, k, l, m) accepted on subagent's per-item disk-verification record per audit MD §1-§16; cross-check was structural rather than disk-based (e.g., n1(j) F.2 mini-soak is a logical replacement validated by reading the dispatch text + LA F.2 disposition).
Sub-clause (n) — Pre-Flight Step 0 rollback-readiness verification — has separate provenance (Guide-#8 dispatch at 9e92b26, NOT the audit subagent); it is itself a runtime verification protocol (5 disk-level checks at execution time) rather than a static-analysis amendment, so the audit-subagent cross-verification framework does not apply. Provenance is documented in §approach + the citation block in <sub_clause id="n">.
independent_disk_verification.per_item_findings_table:
Per-item findings cited from audit MD with §-reference. Class column reflects audit-subagent severity; Disposition column is what's bound in g9-ea9_n1.
| Item | Drift class | Severity | Audit MD § | Bound in | |---|---|---|---|---| | 5.0.5 | NEW_PATTERN (2 missed runtime files) | Class D | §1 | n1(a) | | 5.1 | NONE (cache orphan cosmetic) | Class D | §2 | (no bind; optional cleanup) | | 5.1.v2 | STRUCTURE_MISMATCH + FILE_NOT_FOUND | Class D | §3 | n1(b) | | 5.2 | NONE | N/A | §4 | (no bind) | | 5.3 | NONE for layout; r2 atomicity REAL | Class C | §5, §19 r2 | n1(m) | | 5.4 | STRUCTURE_MISMATCH (3-glob too narrow) | Class C | §6, §18 aug1 | n1(c) | | 5.5 | NEW_PATTERN (3 .md peers missed) | Class D | §7, §18 aug3 | n1(d) | | 5.5.v2 | STRUCTURE_MISMATCH (selective vs wholesale) | **Class A** | §8 | **n1(e) LOAD-BEARING** | | 5.5.5 | STRUCTURE_MISMATCH (path) | Class D | §9 | n1(f) | | 5.6 | STRUCTURE_MISMATCH (description omission) | Class C | §10, §19 r4 | n1(g) | | 5.7 | NEW_PATTERN (vague description) | Class C | §11 | n1(h) | | 5.8 | STRUCTURE_MISMATCH (baseline outdated) | Class C | §12 | n1(i) | | 5.8.v2 | NONE | N/A | §13 | (no bind) | | 5.9 | STRUCTURE_MISMATCH (compound; F.2 not absorbed) | Class C | §14, §20 F.2 | n1(j) | | 5.10 | STRUCTURE_MISMATCH (HALT-gate missing matrix) | Class C | §15, §18 aug2 | n1(k) | | 5.11 | NEW_PATTERN (ordering guard) | Class D | §16 | n1(l) | | 5.12 | NONE (template per Stage 3/4 pattern) | N/A | (covered in EX-4) | (no bind) | | Pre-Flight Step 0 (NEW) | NEW_PATTERN (rollback-readiness gate; not in original dispatch) | Class C | (separate Guide-#8 dispatch at 9e92b26) | n1(n) |
Cross-item cascades CC-1 through CC-5 documented at audit MD §17.

guide_response.required_corrections: NONE for EA-9 retroactively. EA-9 has not begun Stage 5 execution; nothing to correct in committed work. All 14 amendments below apply to EA-9's first-action execution starting at Pre-Flight Step 0 (rollback-readiness verification) → Step 1 (state.pause_fleet on devplatform) → Item 5.0.5.

new_acknowledgment.title: Stage 5.0.v2 dual-track pre-flight audit checkpoint amendments + Guide-#8 rollback-readiness Pre-Flight Step 0 — Items 5.0.5 through 5.11 + 5.3 atomicity + Pre-Flight Step 0 spec-correction binding
new_acknowledgment.preamble:
g9-ea9_n1 is the first ack emitted by Guide-#9 (reviewing EA-9). It binds 14 sub-clauses (a-n) covering all spec corrections surfaced by the Stage 5.0.v2 dual-track audit (Option A subagent + Option B+ inline reconciliation; sub-clauses a-m) PLUS the Pre-Flight Step 0 rollback-readiness verification from Guide-#8 dispatch at 9e92b26 (sub-clause n; added per LA directive 2026-04-29 in lieu of a 4th audit pass). EA-9 must restate g9-ea9_n1 verbatim with all 14 sub-clauses in its iter-1 comprehension gate response BEFORE Pre-Flight execution.
Sub-clause class summary: A=1 (LOAD-BEARING n1(e)), B=0, C=8, D=5; total = 14. The Class A sub-clause (e) is irreversible-operation-grade and converts a wholesale `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force` to a selective 4-of-17 delete preserving 13 BlarAI-internal architectural governance docs per A14 disposition. Sub-clause (n) is the Pre-Flight Step 0 rollback-readiness verification — 5 critical checks (~30s runtime); HARD STOP on any failure; LA decision required for recovery; runs BEFORE state.pause_fleet so failure leaves system clean.

sub_clause.amendment: Extend dispatch line 96 `$patterns` array from `@('*.log', '*.jsonl', '*.json.bak', 'state.json*', '*.history')` to `@('*.log', '*.jsonl', '*.json.bak', 'state.json*', '*.history', 'lifts.json', 'escalation_seen.json')`. Per F.1 Option A "preserves stragglers; minimal cost". Captures 2 runtime artifacts (`tools/autonomy_budget/lifts.json`, `tools/fleet_observability/escalation_seen.json`) that current pattern set misses; ~2 KB forensic metadata per file at archive time.
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §1 + §20 F.1; Workflow-Integrity-Review S-2; F.1 LA disposition.

sub_clause.amendment: Correct dispatch line 14 from `Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\fleet_ops\blarai_next_task_resolver.py` to `Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\autonomy_budget\blarai_next_task_resolver.py`. The resolver lives at `autonomy_budget/`, not `fleet_ops/`, per Workflow-Integrity-Review §4 S-1 + AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW §0.6 R12. Stage 5.1 wholesale-deletes both directories so the verification still passes post-5.1, but for the right reason.

REPLACE dispatch lines 187-189 `` block with the 8-glob expansion per aug1: ``` devplatform/tools/fleet_ops/*.py devplatform/tools/fleet_observability/*.py devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/*.py devplatform/tools/scheduled-tasks/*.ps1 devplatform/tools/scheduled-tasks/*.xml devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp/**/*.py devplatform/tools/gate_stale_cleaner/*.py devplatform/docs/scheduled/wake_templates/*.md ``` AND amend the description with audit-acceptance criteria: "EA-9 verifies via `Select-String -Pattern 'C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI(?!-worktrees)\\tools' -Path <each glob>` AND classifies every live-code hit as either (a) preserve target-project (`-BlarAIRoot` defaults), (b) preserve historical log/test fixture, or (c) DRIFT requiring rewrite. Comment-only hits in `#`-prefix lines are auto-classified preserve unless the comment is doctrinally mis-pointing." Note: All 8 globs are already CLEAN of substantive BlarAI/tools refs per audit MD §18 aug1 verification — but the dispatch's literal 3-glob enumeration is incomplete-by-spec; aug1 closes the scope gap.
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §6, §18 aug1; F.4 dual-track posture; aug1 binding.

sub_clause.amendment:
Extend dispatch lines 199-206 `` block to include 3 additional patterns capturing platform-doctrine `.md` files currently missed: ```
C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\DOMAIN*.md
C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\domain*.md
C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\DEC*.md ``` Captures `DOMAIN8_CLARIFICATIONS_v1.md`, `DOMAIN9_KICKOFF_PROMPT.md`, `domain8_spike_findings.md` (lowercase prefix) per audit MD §7. The `DEC*.md` pattern is forward-compatibility (no current matches; symmetric with `DEC*.xml`).

**REPLACE wholesale `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance` (dispatch line 30) with SELECTIVE delete preserving 13 BlarAI-internal architectural governance docs per A14 disposition.**
Replacement command for the governance portion of Item 5.5.v2: ```powershell $governanceMigrated = @( 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\STYLE.md', 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\fleet-hygiene.md', 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\merge-policy.md', 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md' ) foreach ($p in $governanceMigrated) { if (Test-Path $p) { Remove-Item -Force $p } } ``` KEEP the wake_templates wholesale-delete unchanged (`Remove-Item -Recurse -Force C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\scheduled\wake_templates`) — 6/6 templates were fully migrated at Stage 3.2.v2 per .copy_manifest_v2.yaml.
Update `` line accordingly: `Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance` → expected True (directory + 13 BlarAI-internal docs retained); `Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\scheduled\wake_templates` → expected False.
The 13 BlarAI-internal governance docs preserved by this amendment: README.md (governance index), circuit-breaker.md, configuration-management.md, context-spotlighting.md, deployment-verification.md, error-recovery.md, gpu-runtime.md, ipc-protocol.md, observability.md, pgov-validation.md, rule-engine.md, session-state.md, streaming-output.md.
RATIONALE: the dispatch's description (line 24) claims "Copied to devplatform in Stage 3.2.v2 — now DELETE from BlarAI" — implying 17/17 governance docs were migrated. Reality per A14 + on-disk verification (Glob): only 4/17 migrated (the fleet-relevant subset: STYLE, fleet-hygiene, merge-policy, parallel-sprints). The other 13 are BlarAI-internal architectural governance (USE-CASE policies, ADR templates, hardware/runtime docs) and stay at BlarAI per the explicit Stage 3 selective-copy decision (g7-ea1_n12). Wholesale-delete contradicts that Stage 3 disposition and would cause irreversible data loss.
LOAD-BEARING: if EA-9 cannot reach LA mid-stage at Item 5.5.v2, fall back to selective-delete (preserve the 13). Data-preservation default per "irreversible operations require explicit affirmation."
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §8 (HIGHEST-IMPACT FINDING); STATUS.md A14; Stage 3 close marker (g7-ea1_n12); on-disk Glob verification (Guide-#9 cross-verified).

sub_clause.amendment: Correct dispatch line 42 path-spec from `docs/ea_queue/archive/ — EA per-sprint archives (per-project)` to `scheduled/ea_queue/archive/ — EA per-sprint archives (per-project)`. The actual location is `BlarAI\scheduled\ea_queue\archive\` (top-level `scheduled/`), which Item 5.3 moves to `.platform/scheduled/ea_queue/archive/`. Pure documentation drift; no executable side-effect.

REPLACE dispatch description line 211 ("remove anything referencing tools/vikunja_mcp, tools/fleet_*, tools/autonomy_budget") with explicit enumeration: ``` Edit `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` testpaths array (lines 6-22 of BlarAI/pyproject.toml). REMOVE these 6 testpaths (post-Stage-5.1 deletion of platform tool dirs): - "tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/tests" - "tools/vikunja_mcp/tests" - "tools/autonomy_budget/tests" - "tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests" - "tools/fleet_observability/tests" - "tools/fleet_ops/tests" PRESERVE these 9 testpaths (BlarAI runtime — required for 5.8.v2 baseline): - "shared/tests" - "services/policy_agent/tests" - "services/assistant_orchestrator/tests" - "services/semantic_router/tests" - "services/ui_gateway/tests" - "services/ui_shell/tests" - "launcher/tests" - "tests/integration" - "tools/tests" Markers (lines 24-27): asyncio + slow; no platform-tool refs; NO marker removal needed. ``` Without this explicit enumeration, EA-9 might leave `tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests` in testpaths (dispatch description's "tools/fleet_*" wildcard does NOT match `gate_stale_cleaner`) — post-5.1 deletion of the directory, pytest at 5.8.v2 would emit ImportError/collection failure.
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §10, §19 r4; on-disk Read of BlarAI/pyproject.toml (Guide-#9 cross-verified); r4 binding.

sub_clause.amendment:
REPLACE dispatch description line 215 (vague: "Add `.platform/` contents handling. Typically `.platform/` SHOULD be tracked... but secrets/tokens inside should be gitignored") with concrete `.gitignore` additions to make at Item 5.7: ``` # Wake launcher mutex (per fleet-hygiene §1 Drift Catalogue Pattern A; # same drift class as the UAT2 entries that caused 60+ accumulated # wake_launcher auto-stashes in 2026-04-22/23). Per w20. .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock
# Stage 5.0.5 archive output (large binary; forensic-only) .platform/audit_archive/*.zip .platform/audit_archive/pre_stage5_*/ ``` Track .platform/sprints/, .platform/scheduled/, .platform/active_tasks.yaml, .platform/docs_sprints/, .platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml — these are project state, NOT runtime artifacts. EA-9 verifies post-edit `git status --short` shows the .platform/ contents staged correctly.
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §11; w20 watchpoint; .gitignore on-disk verification (Guide-#9 cross-verified).

sub_clause.amendment:
Mark Item 5.8 (lines 218-221) SUPERSEDED-BY-5.8.v2 in dispatch text. Replace `` line 220 from "Same baseline (755 passed, 2 skipped). Or full scope (835 passed, 2 skipped) if live runtime available" with: "**SUPERSEDED BY 5.8.v2**: ~981 passed, 22 skipped on `pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q`. The historical 755-baseline reference is pre-Sprint-8 and obsolete per CLAUDE.md §Active State + STATUS.md §v2 Banner."
Operationally: EA-9 runs Item 5.8.v2 command (which includes `launcher/` directory and uses regression scope), NOT the v1 5.8 command. If EA-9 runs v1 5.8 first, sees ~981 passed (not 755), it could mis-interpret as +226-test regression-discovery. False alarm; n1(i) prevents.
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §12; CLAUDE.md §Active State; STATUS.md §v2 Banner line 24.

sub_clause.amendment:
REPLACE Item 5.9 (lines 223-231) entirely with the F.2 30-min mini-soak protocol per LA F.2 disposition. Original dispatch references DISABLED `BlarAI_daily_digest` (Phase A 2026-04-29 disabling); task name is wrong format (should be `Daily Digest` Title Case Spaces, not underscore form).
Replacement Item 5.9: ```xml
F.2 disposition (per LA 2026-04-29): replace original BlarAI_daily_digest probe (DISABLED per Phase A) with a 30-min observational soak across 2 full 15-min wake-* cycles post-Item-5.7 commit. Verify all 4 wake-* roles + cadence_monitor (every 5 min) + escalation_watchdog (every 5 min) produce expected silent-success outputs. **Mature-not-minimal upgrade per Phase D soak methodology proportional to Stage 5.**
Write-Host "Starting 30-min mini-soak from $((Get-Date).ToString('HH:mm:ss'))..." $start = Get-Date $deadline = $start.AddMinutes(30) while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline) { Start-Sleep 60 $elapsed = ((Get-Date) - $start).TotalMinutes Write-Host "Elapsed $([math]::Round($elapsed,1)) min..." } Write-Host "`n=== Post-soak fleet state ===" Get-ScheduledTask -TaskPath '\BlarAI\' | Get-ScheduledTaskInfo | Where-Object { $_.LastRunTime -ge $start } | Select TaskName, LastRunTime, LastTaskResult | Format-Table -AutoSize
During the 30-min window, expected firings (cadence × 30 min): - Wake SDO, Wake Co-Lead Architect, Wake EA Code (every 15 min, 2 firings each = 6 total) - Sprint Auditor (every 15 min, 2 firings) - Agents Cadence Monitor (every 5 min, 6 firings) - Escalation Watchdog (every 5 min, 6 firings) - Credentials Rotation Reminder (typically daily; likely won't fire in 30-min window — NOT a failure if absent) - Gate Stale Cleaner (typically hourly; may not fire in 30-min window — NOT a failure if absent) ACCEPTANCE: all tasks that DO fire within the 30-min window show LastTaskResult=0. Tasks that don't fire due to longer cadence (e.g., Credentials Rotation Reminder daily, Gate Stale Cleaner hourly) are NOT a failure condition — only firings are evaluated. Wake-* tasks: WORK-GATE skip + NO-WORK-EXIT (verify via devplatform/tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/<role>.log entries; NO claude.exe child processes). Cadence Monitor + Escalation Watchdog: silent-success markers per Phase D evidence pattern.
``` Per F.2 binding decision; per LA mature-not-minimal motto.
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §14, §20 F.2; F.2 LA disposition (UPGRADED).

sub_clause.amendment: Add precondition guard before Item 5.11 tag commands: ```powershell $onMain = (git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI branch --show-current) -eq 'main' if (-not $onMain) { Write-Warning "Item 5.11 expected to run AFTER 5.10 merge to main. Current branch: $((git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI branch --show-current)). Tagging chore/platform-extraction tip; LA may need to re-tag on main post-merge." } git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI tag post-platform-extract -m "BlarAI cleanup complete, devplatform authoritative" git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform tag platform-extracted -m "devplatform established from BlarAI extraction" ``` Cosmetic only; if LA REJECTS at 5.10 HALT, tags created on chore/platform-extraction tip are still meaningful (close-state marker). If LA APPROVES (typical path), tags land on main via the merge commit. PRESERVE existing `pre-platform-extract` tag (BlarAI side; per RECOVERY.md asset coverage).
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §16; w10 watchpoint (Item 5.10 LA-merge gate).

sub_clause.amendment:
INSERT new "Step 0 — Rollback Readiness Verification" in EA-9 init pointer Pre-Flight section, BEFORE Step 1 (state.pause_fleet on devplatform). Embed the consolidated PowerShell block from Guide-#8 dispatch §check_definition → <consolidated_powershell_block> verbatim (5 checks; ~30s runtime).
The 5 critical checks: 1. `pre-platform-extract` git tag exists on BlarAI (rollback target) 2. Bundle file exists at `C:\Users\mrbla\backups\BlarAI_pre_extract.bundle` 3. OOG (out-of-git) zip exists at `C:\Users\mrbla\backups\blarai_oog_20260424_171721.zip` 4. Bundle SHA256 matches `C051CCFB8FBC6CA31E03F48537816F56CC3FAFC927CEAFAEC9825C9EC65D7707` (Stage 0 manifest) 5. devplatform git log includes Phase C wake_launcher fix at `be22a15`
Step 0 placement BEFORE Step 1 (fleet pause) is intentional: read-only check; failure leaves system clean (no fleet pause to revert, no branch checkout to undo). PASS authorizes EA-9 to proceed to Step 1 (existing fleet-pause SOP per `.github/copilot-instructions.md §fleet_pause_sop`).
Failure handling: any single FAIL → HARD STOP. EA-9 reports to LA via chat with check ID + failure detail + per-check failure_disposition guidance (verbatim from Guide-#8 dispatch §per_check_details). EA-9 does NOT autonomously execute recovery (tag recreation, bundle regeneration, OOG-zip restore, Phase C fix re-application) — those are LA decisions because they involve modifying baseline state.
Output capture: EA-9 captures the inline PowerShell output text block in the Stage 5 close STATUS append (Item 5.12 — under a "Pre-Flight Verification Outputs" subsection per Guide-#8 dispatch §success_criteria).
Optional mature-not-minimal extensions if EA-9 wall-clock budget permits (per Guide-#8 dispatch §optional_extensions; +15-25s runtime; same HARD STOP discipline): - ext1: OOG zip SHA256 verification against `15B4B9A138974F500FFAE9682350F333DC228AC94A1DB184E26A220508A129A8` - ext2: 13 scheduled task XML backups exist at `C:\Users\mrbla\backups\scheduled_tasks_export\` - ext3: devplatform branch is `main` (not branched off mid-Stage) LA may direct EA-9 to include or skip these extensions in iter-1 comprehension gate.
sub_clause.citation: Guide-#8 dispatch `5.0-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_8_ROLLBACK_READINESS_PREFLIGHT_FOR_EA_9.xml` (committed at 9e92b26); LA directive 2026-04-29 (rollback-readiness verification chosen instead of 4th audit pass; mature-not-minimal procedural step in lieu of additional audit pass given diminishing returns); STATUS.md Recovery Anchors table; STATUS.md Stage 4 Closure Footnotes §4 (Phase C remediation) + Anomaly A19 (BUG 1 fix at devplatform be22a15).

guide_response.required_acknowledgments:
EA-9 must explicitly acknowledge ALL items below VERBATIM in iter-1 comprehension gate response BEFORE Pre-Flight execution.
I will restate the cumulative-ack chain VERBATIM (116 atoms inherited: g7-ea1_n10..n13 + g7-ea7_n14 + g8-ea8_n1(a..p) + g8-ea8_n2(a..f) + g8-ea8_n3(a..l)) per dn5/dn22/s9w1. I will restate g9-ea9_n1 VERBATIM with all 14 sub-clauses (a) through (n), including the verbatim text of n1(e) Class A LOAD-BEARING amendment converting Item 5.5.v2 wholesale-delete to selective 4-of-17 + n1(n) Pre-Flight Step 0 rollback-readiness verification per Guide-#8 dispatch at 9e92b26. I will pause the fleet via `state.pause_fleet('Stage 5 cleanup', updated_by='ea_code_blarai_helper', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')` then commit `chore(ops): pause fleet -- Stage 5 cleanup` BEFORE any branch checkout / non-trivial edit, per `.github/copilot-instructions.md §fleet_pause_sop`. The state.json target is **devplatform/** (NOT BlarAI/; BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json is a dead artifact post-Phase-C4 per w21+dn31; auto-resolved at Stage 5.1 directory deletion per Ticket KK). I will NOT pop or apply ANY wake_launcher auto-stash entry per dn10 + w8 (corrected per A20). Current stash list verified at audit time: stash@{0} co_lead_architect 20260429_004502, stash@{1} sdo 20260429_004501; both are wake_launcher auto-stashes; discipline is generic regardless of timestamp. I will NOT delete `BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` directly per dn31 + w21. Stage 5.1 deletes the parent directory which auto-resolves it. I will NOT execute Stage 5.1 deletions before Item 5.0.5 audit archive completes per dn32. Item ordering: 5.0.5 → 5.1. I will NOT edit BlarAI's CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, or AGENTS.md per dn3 (V8 / 6.1.v2 deferred). I will NOT merge `chore/platform-extraction` to `main` until LA explicitly approves per R10 + w10. Item 5.10 has explicit HALT for this; n1(k) extends with review matrix. **LOAD-BEARING per n1(e)**: I will execute n1(e)'s selective-delete amendment at Item 5.5.v2 (preserving 13 BlarAI-internal governance docs per A14), NOT the original dispatch wholesale-delete. If at execution time the dispatch text appears unamended, I STOP and verify with LA before proceeding. Data-preservation default if LA unreachable: selective-delete. I will spot-check Item 5.12 STATUS.md append for duplication BEFORE committing per d9 + A17. Use `Add-Content -NoNewline` or hash-verified append helper if available; otherwise post-write hash-verification. Guide-#9 will spawn 3 parallel verification subagents at Stage 5 close (per d6 + w14). I cooperate by providing requested artifacts (file lists, git diff outputs) but do NOT manage subagent design. Independent disk-level verification by Guide-#9 happens AFTER subagents emit; my role at close is comprehensive close-report cross-checked by Guide-#9 per w11/dn14. Cross-check discipline: my "I did X" reports must be backed by independent `git diff` / `Test-Path` / `Get-ChildItem` evidence. EA-paraphrase-introduced-error pattern (w11) is a Guide-#9 cross-verification standard; close-report under-enumeration triggers re-verify per dn14/dn20/dn24. I will run Step 0 (rollback-readiness verification) BEFORE state.pause_fleet. If any of the 5 checks fail, I HARD STOP and report to LA with the failure_disposition guidance from the dispatch. I do NOT autonomously execute recovery (tag recreation, bundle regeneration, etc.) — those are LA decisions.
guide_response.proceed_authorization:
HOLD — re-emit your iter-1 comprehension gate summary acknowledging the 13 required_acknowledgments above + restating the full 116-atom chain + g9-ea9_n1 14 sub-clauses verbatim, then await Guide-#9 re-review for PROCEED authorization. Estimated EA-9 iter-1 turn count to PROCEED: 1-2 (depending on completeness of restatement).
PROCEED THRESHOLD: all 13 acknowledgments present in EA-9's response + 14 sub-clauses restated verbatim + 116-atom chain restated verbatim. Class A n1(e) acknowledgment (ack9, LOAD-BEARING; selective-vs-wholesale disposition) AND Class C n1(n) acknowledgment (ack13; Step 0 rollback-readiness HARD STOP discipline) are both blocking acknowledgments; PROCEED is contingent on both being present and unambiguous.
